ChunkMeta.description:
Sourcing metadata attached to every chunk.

``extra='allow'`` is intentional: parsers can attach freeform debug
info (e.g. Docling element IDs) without forcing a schema change.

extracted_by.description: Identifier(s) of the parser(s) that produced this chunk, with versions — e.g. 'docling@2.x, pymupdf@1.24'.
extracted_by.title: Extracted By

parser_confidence.description: Optional 0..1 confidence score from the parser, if it exposes one. Used by validation; not interpreted by downstream stages.
parser_confidence.title: Parser Confidence

source_spans.description: One or more regions of the PDF that produced this chunk. Concatenating chunk.text[span.start:span.end] for each span in order yields the chunk's text.
source_spans.title: Source Spans

ChunkMeta.title: ChunkMeta

ChunkType.description:
Kind of element a chunk represents.

Drives validation rules (e.g. tables must carry an ``asset_path``)
and is part of every ``chunk_id``. See ``docs/schemas.md`` §Chunk types
for when each value is used.
ChunkType.title: ChunkType

Reference.description:
One outbound cross-reference from a chunk to a target.

Produced by Stage 3 (``resolve_references``). Internal references that
couldn't be resolved keep ``target_chunk_id=None`` and surface in the
validation report as resolution-rate misses, not schema errors.

external_id.description: Normalised identifier for external references — e.g. 'BS-EN-13501-1:2018'. None for internal references.
external_id.title: External Id

label.description: Surface text as it appeared in the source — e.g. 'paragraph 2.4', 'Diagram 3.1', 'BS EN 13501-1:2018'.
label.title: Label

pattern_name.description: Name of the config regex pattern that fired (None if the reference came from a PDF hyperlink). Useful for debugging pattern coverage.
pattern_name.title: Pattern Name

source.description: How the reference was discovered. Hyperlink-derived references are preferred over pattern-derived ones during deduplication.
source.title: Source

source_span.description: Where in the source PDF the reference's surface text appears. Useful for highlighting the citation in a UI.

target_chunk_id.description: chunk_id this reference points at. None when the target is external (a BS standard, another Approved Document) or when an internal reference couldn't be resolved.
target_chunk_id.title: Target Chunk Id

type.description: The kind of edge this reference represents.

Reference.title: Reference

ReferenceType.description:
Kind of edge between a chunk and its target.

See ``docs/schemas.md`` §References for guidance on which type to use.
ReferenceType.title: ReferenceType

SourceSpan.description:
One contiguous region of one page that produced part of a chunk.

A chunk can have multiple spans: one paragraph wrapping across a page
break would have two; one wrapping across two columns would have two
on the same page. Together, the spans give exact, lossless provenance
from a chunk's text back to the source PDF.

bbox.description: Bounding box (x0, y0, x1, y1) in PDF points, top-left origin. x1 > x0 and y1 > y0 are enforced.
bbox.title: Bbox

page.description: 1-indexed page number in the source PDF.
page.title: Page

text_offset_end.description: Exclusive offset into chunk.text where this span's substring ends. Must be strictly greater than text_offset_start.
text_offset_end.title: Text Offset End

text_offset_start.description: Inclusive offset into chunk.text where this span's substring begins.
text_offset_start.title: Text Offset Start

SourceSpan.title: SourceSpan

description:
The core node of the output graph.

A chunk is one self-contained, addressable piece of a document — a
numbered paragraph, a heading, a table, a diagram, a caption, a
glossary entry. Everything else in the contract is either an edge
between chunks (``references_out``, ``parent_section_id``,
``caption_target_id``), provenance on the chunk (``meta``), or an
index over chunks (``TOC``, ``ReferencesIndex``).

See ``docs/schemas.md`` §Chunk for a field-by-field plain-English guide.

asset_path.description: Path to the extracted binary asset, relative to output/<doc_id>/ — e.g. 'assets/diagram-3.1.png'. Required for tables and diagrams.
asset_path.title: Asset Path

asset_sidecar_path.description: Path to a JSON sidecar describing the asset (e.g. table cells), if any. None when the binary alone is sufficient.
asset_sidecar_path.title: Asset Sidecar Path

attributes.description: Document-specific tags that don't fit the core schema — e.g. {'requirement': ['B1'], 'applies_to': ['dwellinghouse']}. The only schema escape hatch on the chunk itself.
attributes.title: Attributes

caption_target_id.description: When this chunk is a caption: the chunk_id of the table/diagram it captions. Required for type=caption.
caption_target_id.title: Caption Target Id

captioned_by_id.description: When this chunk is captioned: the chunk_id of its caption. Bidirectional with caption_target_id.
captioned_by_id.title: Captioned By Id

chunk_id.description: Globally unique identifier in the form '<doc_id>-<type>-<identifier>'. Stable across pipeline runs on the same PDF + config.
chunk_id.title: Chunk Id

defined_terms_used.description: Normalised glossary terms that appear (whole-word, case-insensitive) in this chunk's text. Back-filled by Stage 5.
defined_terms_used.title: Defined Terms Used

doc_id.description: The document this chunk belongs to (matches Config.doc_id).
doc_id.title: Doc Id

heading_level.description: Required when type is section_heading or appendix; gives the nesting depth (1 is outermost). None for non-heading chunks.
heading_level.title: Heading Level

meta.description: Provenance: which PDF region(s) produced this chunk, by which parser. Always populated.

order_index.description: The chunk's position in the document's reading order. Unique, 0..N-1 across the document. THE single authoritative ordering — never sort by page+bbox at query time.
order_index.title: Order Index

page_end.description: 1-indexed page number where this chunk ends (inclusive; equal to page_start for a single-page chunk).
page_end.title: Page End

page_start.description: 1-indexed page number where this chunk begins.
page_start.title: Page Start

parent_section_id.description: chunk_id of the immediately-enclosing section heading. None only for top-level headings.
parent_section_id.title: Parent Section Id

references_out.description: Outbound cross-references discovered by Stage 3. Empty for chunks that don't cite anything. THE source of truth for every edge from this chunk.
references_out.title: References Out

section_path.description: Human-readable labels of every section heading above this chunk, outermost first — e.g. ['B1', 'Means of warning…', '2 Fire alarms']. For display only. Never label-match against this for retrieval.
section_path.title: Section Path

section_path_ids.description: chunk_ids of the section-heading chunks corresponding to each label in section_path. Same length as section_path. Use these for 'is X under section Y' queries.
section_path_ids.title: Section Path Ids

type.description: What kind of element this chunk represents.

title: Chunk
